title: Velvety Moroccan Almond Tagine
description: A comforting, herbal plantbased tagine with a silky almond butter sauce, sweet apricots, and fragrant spices, perfect for a nostalgic Moroccan feast.

ingredients.name: Spice blend
ingredients.notes: cumin 1 tsp, coriander 1 tsp, smoked paprika 1 tsp, cinnamon 1/2 tsp, ginger 1/4 tsp, turmeric 1 tsp, salt 1/2 tsp, black pepper 1/4 tsp

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed pot or tagine over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic; sauté until fragrant and translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the root vegetables and red bell pepper, stirring to coat with oil and aromatics.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a gentle boil.
Stir in the almond butter, tomato paste, and lemon juice until fully incorporated and the sauce thickens.
Season with the spice blend, adjusting salt and pepper to taste.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30–35 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and the flavors meld.
Fold in the chopped dried apricots, reserving a few for garnish if desired.
Simmer uncovered for an additional 5 minutes so the sauce coats the vegetables.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro, and optionally a drizzle of almond butter or a sprinkling of chopped almonds for extra texture.
